sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: Local councils can arrange practical support to help those caring for elderly people at home. This can give the carer: • time for other responsibilities • va (1) {{BLANK}} Assessment of mother’s needs This may include discussion of: • how much (2) {{BLANK}} the caring involves • what types of tasks are involved, e.g. o help with dressing o helping her have a (3) {{BLANK}} o shopping o helping with meals o dealing with (4) {{BLANK}} • any aspects of caring that are especially difficult, e.g. • loss of (5) {{BLANK}} • (6) {{BLANK}} her • preventing a (7) {{BLANK}} Types of support that may be offered to carers • transport costs, e.g. cost of a (8) {{BLANK}} • car-related costs, e.g. fuel and (9) {{BLANK}} • help with housework • help to reduce (10) {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 11-16: What is the role of the volunteers in each of the following activities? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 11-16. Questions 17-20: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: walking around the town centre {{BLANK}}
options: A providing entertainment
B providing publicity about a council service
C contacting local businesses
D giving advice to visitors
E collecting feedback on events
F selling tickets
G introducing guest speakers at an event
H encouraging cooperation between local organisations
I helping people find their seats

questions.content: Which event requires the largest number of volunteers?
options: A the music festival
B the science festival
C the book festival

questions.content: What is the most important requirement for volunteers at the festivals?
options: A interpersonal skills
B personal interest in the event
C flexibility

questions.content: New volunteers will start working in the week beginning
options: A 2 September.
B 9 September.
C 23 September.

questions.content: What is the next annual event for volunteers?
options: A a boat trip
B a barbecue
C a party

sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: What is Rosie and Colin’s opinion about each of the following aspects of human geography? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 21-25. Questions 26-30: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: Rosie says that in her own city the main problem is
options: A crime.
B housing.
C unemployment.

questions.content: What recent additions to the outskirts of their cities are both students happy about?
options: A conference centres
B sports centres
C retail centres

questions.content: The students agree that developing disused industrial sites may
options: A have unexpected costs.
B damage the urban environment.
C destroy valuable historical buildings.

questions.content: The students will mention Masdar City as an example of an attempt to achieve
options: A daily collections for waste recycling.
B sustainable energy use.
C free transport for everyone.

questions.content: When discussing the Eco town of Greenhill Abbots, Colin is uncertain about
options: A what its objectives were.
B why there was opposition to it.
C how much of it has actually been built.

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Developing food trends
• The growth in interest in food fashions started with (31) {{BLANK}} of food being shared on social media.
• The UK food industry is constantly developing products which are new or different.
• Influencers on social media become ‘ambassadors’ for a brand.
• Sales of (32) {{BLANK}} food brands have grown this way.
• Supermarkets track demand for ingredients on social media.
• Famous (33) {{BLANK}} are influential.

Marketing campaigns
• The avocado:
  o (34) {{BLANK}} were invited to visit growers in South Africa.
  o Advertising focused on its (35) {{BLANK}} benefits.
• Oat milk:
  o A Swedish brand’s media campaign received publicity by upsetting competitors.
  o Promotion in the USA through (36) {{BLANK}} shops reduced the need for advertising.
  o It appealed to consumers who are concerned about the (37) {{BLANK}}
• Norwegian skrei:
  o has helped strengthen the (38) {{BLANK}} of Norwegian seafood.

Ethical concerns
• Quinoa:
  o Its success led to an increase in its (39) {{BLANK}}
  o Overuse of resources resulted in poor quality (40) {{BLANK}}

questions.content: Developing food trends • The growth in interest in food fashions started with (31) {{BLANK}} of food being shared on social media. • The UK food industry is constantly developing products which are new or different. • Influencers on social media become ‘ambassadors’ for a brand. • Sales of (32) {{BLANK}} food brands have grown this way. • Supermarkets track demand for ingredients on social media. • Famous (33) {{BLANK}} are influential. Marketing campaigns • The avocado: o (34) {{BLANK}} were invited to visit growers in South Africa. o Advertising focused on its (35) {{BLANK}} benefits. • Oat milk: o A Swedish brand’s media campaign received publicity by upsetting competitors. o Promotion in the USA through (36) {{BLANK}} shops reduced the need for advertising. o It appealed to consumers who are concerned about the (37) {{BLANK}} • Norwegian skrei: o has helped strengthen the (38) {{BLANK}} of Norwegian seafood. Ethical concerns • Quinoa: o Its success led to an increase in its (39) {{BLANK}} o Overuse of resources resulted in poor quality (40) {{BLANK}}
